test.name: Convert Quaker numbered-month date
test.description: Convert a Quaker-style date with numbered months to standard calendar format. Two subtleties: Quakers numbered months to avoid pagan names, but the numbering changed in 1752 when England adopted the Gregorian calendar (before 1752, the year started in March; after, in January). The date is 1845, post-1752, so 2nd month = February. The skill should explain the convention, apply the post-1752 numbering, and present both forms.

input.user_message: Convert this Quaker date: '3rd day of 2nd month 1845'. What date is this in the standard calendar?

additional_criteria: Should explain the Quaker numbering convention (months and days numbered to avoid pagan names — January = 1st month post-1752, March = 1st month pre-1752)
Should note that the convention CHANGED in 1752 when England adopted the Gregorian calendar; before 1752, 2nd month = April (year started in March); after 1752, 2nd month = February
Should correctly apply the post-1752 numbering for an 1845 date and produce 'February 3, 1845' (or '3 Feb 1845')
Should preserve the original Quaker notation in its output so a future genealogist can verify the conversion — not silently replacing it
